steps.title: Link language to plant knowledge
steps.content: The passage says medicinal plant knowledge is reflected in Indigenous language vocabulary. If ancestral languages fade, that knowledge is at risk, so tribal nations preserving those languages also preserve the knowledge encoded in them. The final sentence asks what else language preservation accomplishes.

steps.title: Reject claims beyond language
steps.content: Choice A says preservation increases the number of plants represented, but the passage is about maintaining existing knowledge. Choice B shifts to globally dominant languages. Choice D concerns physical access to plants, not vocabulary or knowledge. The answer is C.
